steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Find the circumference
steps.content: The radius is $2$, so the circumference is $$2\pi r=2\pi(2)=4\pi.$$

steps.title: Divide arc length by circumference
steps.content: The fraction of the circumference is $$\frac{\pi/2}{4\pi}=\frac{\pi}{2}\cdot\frac{1}{4\pi}=\frac{1}{8}.$$

steps.title: Confirm accepted forms
steps.content: The answer is 1/8. Decimal entries like $.125$ or $0.125$ are also accepted. The fraction is less than one full circle because the arc is only part of the circumference.
